items.title: From adoption to impact: Three horizons of AI transformation
items.summary: Most organizations are still early in their AI journeys. A new global survey reveals how companies can progress from individual adoption to enterprise-wide value capture.

items.title: Cost versus value: Managing agentic AI system performance
items.summary: Per-token pricing has stopped being a useful measure for what enterprises actually pay for gen AI. A conversation with David Tepper, CEO of Pay-i, delves into the new economics of measurement.

items.title: From healthcare to health: Asia’s longevity opportunity
items.summary: On average, people are living longer than before, raising a broader conversation about how to create an ecosystem that effectively combines the delivery of care with proven ways to prevent illness.

items.title: Reimagining logistics pricing
items.summary: Logistics players can focus on four pricing strategies in the AI era.

items.title: The operating model advantage: Why AI winners are rewiring their organizations
items.summary: The real AI advantage comes from redesigning how work is done and how decisions are made. Ultimately, success will depend on people and operating models supported by technology.

items.title: From the C-suite to the boardroom: Christiana Smith Shi on what it takes
items.summary: For executives, the developmental opportunity may be clear, but what does board service really entail?

items.title: Why accelerated resource allocation matters in the age of AI
items.summary: In an era of rapid AI disruption, the winners may not be the companies that predict the future most accurately, but those that learn, reallocate resources, and adapt faster than their competitors.

items.title: Building America’s innovation engine: An interview with David Lebryk
items.summary: The former US Treasury official shares how government can deliver in moments of crisis and how America’s public–private sector dynamic can help fuel its next era of growth.

items.title: A microscope on small businesses: Chile’s productivity opportunity
items.summary: Overcoming scale-related barriers and strengthening ties with other firms—both large and small—can help small businesses in Chile maximize their potential and narrow the productivity gap.

items.title: How Covista is changing education for healthcare professionals
items.summary: Chairman and CEO Steve Beard explains how the organization’s multiyear transformation has reshaped its purpose, structure, and culture.
